# RoleModel Craftsmanship Radar

The Craftsmanship Radar defines the progression path at RoleModel Software across six dimensions: Experience, Collaboration, Critical Thinking, Ownership, Growth Focus, and Discipline.

---

## Apprentice Phase

### Novice
**Overview:** Ready for Immersive Learning
**Avg Time in Level:** 1-3 months | **Total Time:** 1-3 months

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Self-directed/self-motivated learning, 2+ examples of self-directed design work, bonus: project experience in a related field (e.g. as a programmer on a custom software project) |
| **Collaboration** | You give them structured assignments for learning |
| **Critical Thinking** | Can follow recipes and do basic troubleshooting (search errors on Stack Overflow, etc.) |
| **Ownership** | |
| **Growth Focus** | |
| **Discipline** | Shows basic reliability and follows through on simple commitments with structure |
| **Recommended Reading** | |

---

### Apprentice
**Overview:** Net positive on a project, actively learning with help
**Avg Time in Level:** 3-6 months | **Total Time:** 4-9 months

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Completed immersion. Familiar with basic tools and processes. Demonstrated ability to productively apply skills on 2+ assignments with at least 1 being real-world (a real product with real users — can be an internal project) |
| **Collaboration** | You hand them a fully broken down, simple task and they can deliver it solo with review. In pairing sessions they aren't lost, but are not expected to drive much |
| **Critical Thinking** | |
| **Ownership** | Owns their growth and puts in the effort to be more effective |
| **Growth Focus** | Seeks opportunities to grow in every project. Active professional development outside of projects |
| **Discipline** | Comes prepared and completes daily tasks reliably without reminders |
| **Recommended Reading** | Apprenticeship Patterns |

---

### Resident
**Overview:** Takes on solo tasks and follows patterns in the system
**Avg Time in Level:** 3-9 months | **Total Time:** 7-18 months

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Consistent performance on given real-world tasks with minimal assistance. Consistently taking tasks from beginning to end (including asking questions, reviewing with stakeholders, demoing at iteration meetings, and moving to done). At least 1 project performing well in a customer-facing role |
| **Collaboration** | They can take on straightforward tasks, but will need help getting unblocked. You need a short feedback loop to ensure they're going in the right direction |
| **Critical Thinking** | **Uses BRAVE framework to deliver value** |
| **Ownership** | Owns a given task to completion |
| **Growth Focus** | Embraces challenges as an opportunity to learn |
| **Discipline** | Stays organized and focused, plans daily work, and self-corrects quickly when commitments slip |
| **Recommended Reading** | Atomic Habits |

---

### Sr. Resident
**Overview:** Effective troubleshooting and delivery of interdependent tasks
**Avg Time in Level:** 3-9 months | **Total Time:** 9 months - 2 years

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Customer-facing role on at least 1 real project through multiple productive iterations. Track record of fielding new information and getting craftsman input at the right times. 4+ TDD-driven implementations of significant features |
| **Collaboration** | You point in the right direction on straightforward tasks. They can follow patterns in the system or pull from similar projects. They are able to engage the customer in productive conversation during standups for feedback |
| **Critical Thinking** | Effectively works through debugging/troubleshooting tasks |
| **Ownership** | |
| **Growth Focus** | Shares learning with others (#til, #solutions, talks) |
| **Discipline** | Manages multi-day work independently and keeps progress visible without prompting |
| **Recommended Reading** | Impact Players |

---

## Journeyman Phase

### Developer I
**Overview:** Solid delivery on any project and can solve reproducible bugs
**Avg Time in Level:** 1-2 years | **Total Time:** 2-3 years

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Significant contributor on 2+ projects, Explore Phase experience, and experience implementing in a production environment |
| **Collaboration** | You set guardrails for the task and help them break it down. They directly interact with the customer as needed |
| **Critical Thinking** | Can figure out straightforward tasks that require some research |
| **Ownership** | |
| **Growth Focus** | |
| **Discipline** | Delivers steady, predictable progress and consistently meets commitments on time |
| **Recommended Reading** | Crucial Conversations |

---

### Developer II
**Overview:** Proactive breakdown of tasks; asking the right questions at the right time
**Avg Time in Level:** 1-2 years | **Total Time:** 3-5 years

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Significant contributor on 2+ significant projects, at least one greenfield. Significant involvement on 4+ other projects |
| **Collaboration** | You can give them ill-defined tasks and they can pull the information needed from the customer or another source to deliver well and not get blocked |
| **Critical Thinking** | Makes the right tradeoffs to balance personal productivity within project constraints |
| **Ownership** | Can run a standup and understands the bigger project goals |
| **Growth Focus** | Manages their own professional development, showing they are ready to take on a mentee |
| **Discipline** | Proactively plans work, avoids surprises, and ensures clear and focused follow-through with collaborators |
| **Recommended Reading** | Coaching Habit |

---

### Developer III
**Overview:** Anticipates issues with a solution, understands architectural tradeoffs
**Avg Time in Level:** 1-2 years | **Total Time:** 4-7 years

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Significant contributor on 4+ significant projects. Significant involvement on 6+ other projects |
| **Collaboration** | They own a piece of the system and collaborate with Craftsman on functionality and technical implementation (architecture, design, code review). Able to context switch between projects productively |
| **Critical Thinking** | Can troubleshoot problems in systems they haven't worked in |
| **Ownership** | Can own a piece of the system because of trust built with Craftsman |
| **Growth Focus** | Proactively sees the challenges within team/project and seeks resources/solutions to solve them |
| **Discipline** | Sets the team's discipline standard by anticipating risks and keeping workstreams organized and steady |
| **Recommended Reading** | Extreme Ownership |

---

### Sr. Developer
**Overview:** Patterns focused, taking on project ownership, balancing multiple projects
**Avg Time in Level:** 2-4 years | **Total Time:** 6-11 years

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | At least 1 successful project as Tech Lead on a significant project, significant contribution on 2+ significant projects with systems in production |
| **Collaboration** | They are self-directed within the project goals as co-leader of the project and can lead the project in the temporary absence of a Craftsman |
| **Critical Thinking** | Thinks about issues at a higher level and uses small experiments to iteratively arrive at the best solutions |
| **Ownership** | Co-ownership of the project |
| **Growth Focus** | Focus has shifted from just technical to team/people growth |
| **Discipline** | Shapes and maintains disciplined team rhythms, preventing chaos through preparation, focus, and accountability |
| **Recommended Reading** | |

---

## Craftsman Phase

### Craftsman
**Overview:** Breaking a project down and overseeing a team to deliver it
**Total Time:** 9-15 years

| Dimension | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Experience** | Led 2+ successful projects from Explore to Support, significant involvement on 8+ projects, mentored 1 or more developers |
| **Collaboration** | Facilitates productive collaboration between all parties on the project (internal/external). Keeps the focus on the business goals |
| **Critical Thinking** | Can lead an Explore to build the right walking skeleton and produce a roadmap that maps out the needed functionality to achieve customer goals |
| **Ownership** | Balances personal and team productivity to meet project goals and timeline. Ultimately responsible for the success of the project |
| **Growth Focus** | Continuous improvement as a leader that can productively grow people |
| **Discipline** | Models organization-wide disciplined leadership and reinforces systems that elevate discipline across teams |
| **Recommended Reading** | |

---

## Definitions

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| **Significant project** | Explore to Production of a greenfield project; or a multiple-iteration effort to provide significantly improved capabilities or processes |
| **Successful project** | Delivered on time with a happy customer who wants to do more work with us |
| **Simple task** | Anyone who knows the related technology can potentially diagnose and/or do it: e.g. changing calculations, text, manner of display, adding new properties to an object and supporting methods around it |
| **Straightforward task** | Solution requires new classes and non-trivial methods spanning multiple domain objects (and corresponding controllers/views), consistency and responsiveness of user interface, tests written well and handling edge cases, but with clear steps to take for the implementation |
| **Significant contributor** | Key team member completing a lot of work on the project over a significant amount of time |
